svg.text: PORTUGUESE (PORTUGUÊS) / TAMAZIGHT (ⵜⴰⵎⴰⵣⵉⵖⵜ)
INDO-EUROPEAN / AFROASIATIC · ATLANTIC NEIGHBORS · ACROSS THE STRAIT
PLATE 27A ALT · SCRIPT & SOUNDS
— PORTUGUESE: SCRIPT & PHONOLOGY OVERVIEW —
Latin alphabet · orthography reflects historical pronunciation more than current
Diacritics: ã à â á é ê í ó ô ú ü ç — nasal vowels (unique in Romance)
European Portuguese vs Brazilian Portuguese: significant pronunciation difference
NASAL VOWELS: PORTUGUESE SIGNATURE FEATURE
/ã/ (masc. pl. suffix) · /õ/ (they, from Latin -unt) · /ɛ̃/ /ĩ/ /ʉ̃/
pão (bread) /pɐ̃w/ · irmã (sister) · atenção (attention)
Nasal vowels: unique to Portuguese among major Romance languages
EUROPEAN vs BRAZILIAN PRONUNCIATION
European: vowel reduction/deletion in unstressed syllables (prato = /pratu/)
Brazilian: full vowel quality preserved (prato = /pra.tu/) — more open
Both: same orthography; mutual intelligibility high in writing, moderate in speech
PORTUGUESE CONSONANTS
/p b t d k g/ · /f v s z ʃ ʒ/ · /m n ɲ/ · /l ʎ/ · /r ʁ ɾ/
Palatal sounds: lh /ʎ/ (filho=son) · nh /ɲ/ (vinho=wine) — from Latin groups
Sibilants: s/z before vowels; sh/zh before consonants (European) or s/z (Brazilian)
SAMPLE WORDS
olá (hello) · obrigado/a (thank you) · água (água) = water
saudàde (longing/nostalgia) — the Portuguese untranslatable
São Paulo (Saint Paul) · ã = nasal vowel in city name
PORTUGUESE AROUND THE WORLD
~260M speakers · official in: Portugal, Brazil, Angola, Mozambique, Cape Verde
Also: Guinea-Bissau, São Tomé and Príncipe, East Timor, Macau, Equatorial Guinea
TAMAZIGHT (ⵜⴰⵎⴰⵣⵉⵖⵜ TAMAZIGHT) — MOROCCO/ALGERIA · ~550MI
— TAMAZIGHT: THREE SCRIPTS —
TIFINAGH: ancient Berber script; modern Neo-Tifinagh standardized by IRCAM (2003)
Morocco official: Neo-Tifinagh ⵜⴰⵎⴰⵣⵉⵖⵜ for official Amazigh language
Latin (Kabyle Algeria, diaspora, online) · Arabic (some communities)
TIFINAGH SCRIPT: ANCIENT & REVIVED
Tifinagh: 2,000+ years old · found in rock inscriptions across North Africa
Tuareg still use traditional Tifinagh (Tamahaq) — mostly women preserved it
Neo-Tifinagh: Unicode block U+2D30–U+2D7F · added to Unicode 4.1 (2005)
TAMAZIGHT VARIETIES
Tachelhit/Shilha: ~8M, SW Morocco (High Atlas, Souss) — largest variety
Central Atlas Tamazight: ~3M, Atlas Mountains, central Morocco
Tarifit/Rif Berber: ~1.5M, Rif mountains, N Morocco — closest to Iberia
Kabyle: ~5M, Kabylie, NE Algeria — most documented; diaspora in France
TAMAZIGHT PHONOLOGY OVERVIEW
3 short vowels: /a i u/ · some dialects: schwa /ə/ in consonant clusters
Rich consonant inventory: pharyngeals /ħ ʕ/ · uvulars /q ʁ/ · emphatics /ʂ ẓ ṭ ẑ/
SAMPLE WORDS (TIFINAGH + LATIN)
ⵜⴰⵎⴰⵣⵉⵖⵜ = tamazight (the language/the free language)
azul (hello in Kabyle) · aman (water) · tanemmirt (thank you, Tachelhit)
amazigh = free person (origin of Berber identity term)
